title: fumaria parviflora var. glauca
intro: Fumaria parviflora var. glauca, also known as small-flowered fumitory or glaucous fumitory, is a plant species within the fumitory family (Fumariaceae), characterized by its glaucous leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative and diuretic due to its high content of anthraquinones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Fumaria parviflora var. glauca include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve urinary function. Fumaria parviflora var. glauca can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
